channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Hugo Martin on Doom: The Dark Ages DLC: 'We Wanted to Make This Content Since Doom 2016'
item.content:encoded:
2025 was stacked with a lot of great games, so it's a shame that a game with such pedigree behind it—Doom: The Dark Ages—flew a bit under the radar. With its upcoming DLC called Doom: The Dark Ages | Revelations, now is the perfect time to take the leap and rip and tear the forces of Hell.

Doom: The Dark Ages | Revelations offers a frankly staggering amount of new content. Said to be larger than Doom Eternal's DLC combined, Revelations gives players new weapons to fight with, a range of missions to conquer, and even Metroidvania-esque gameplay design thrown into the mix. 

TechRaptor attended an online press preview event for Doom: The Dark Ages | Revelations, including insight from developers Hugo Martin and Marty Stratton during a group Q&A session. See below for all the meaty details on this DLC, coming out on July 7.

All the New Content Coming to Doom: The Dark Ages | Revelations

With a total of six levels to battle through, Doom: The Dark Ages | Revelations is built around a brand new weapon called the Chain Spear. Guns will remain largely the same, according to Martin, but the Chain Spear is where much of the progression and gameplay is focused. 

The Chain Spear is a melee weapon, wielded in the left hand much like the Gauntlet and Shield from the base game. Like other melee weapons, it has multiple functions and uses. In some gameplay footage, we see the Doom Slayer throwing a spear at some unmarked terrain, allowing the player to get extra height and jump into battle headfirst. 

The Spear can be thrown for a high-precision, powerful shot, but it also grapples onto enemies. Once lodged in larger enemies, you're able to orbit around them in the air, adding some verticality to Doom: The Dark Ages. This tool has a ton of nuance, promises Martin, even if it feels weaker than the Shield at the start. 

"It's not until end game that you really really start to see the depth and experience, the power that the spear has," said Martin. "And I think it's equal to the Shield for for sure, but early on in the first level when you get it, obviously it's a new tool without all all of the progress and upgrades unlocked yet."

Progression in the DLC is built around the Spear, and some its most power unlocks are only achievable in the end-game, giving players a great incentive to discover just how far they can go with this new weapon. Outside of throwing it, it slashes through enemy armor and seems to be an extremely versatile weapon, perhaps even more so than the others.

We also get a look at some new enemies in Doom: The Dark Ages | Revelations, including exploding zombies and Hell Knights, but also a Wizard. The Wizard is a floating entity that shoots dark orbs, but there are sure to be more enemies we've yet to see. As for bosses, we learned about an "Uber Boss" which acts as part of Revelations' substantial end-game, post-campaign activities.

Outside of just giving players new story missions, Doom: The Dark Ages | Revelations seems to place an emphasis on challenges for the most seasoned players. That includes the Uber Boss, which requires players to engage with end-game content to put together a key in order to access. Defeating the Uber Boss then unlocks "Master Arenas," which are four separate areas offering new, deadly combat challenges.

Exploration is also another key component to this DLC, just as it was in the base game. Large, open spaces with secrets and resources are there for players to collect, and the Hub adds onto that. 

"The Eternal Hub was great," said Martin, "but that's more like a stepping stone to what's on display here. We have some Metroidvania-esque elements, puzzles, hidden corridors. The space really expands around you and we make it worth your time to explore."

Perhaps most intriguing are the retro elements thrown into the mix, which aims to expand upon the Doom Slayer's lore by looking back into the past. We get glimpses of old-school Doom levels re-made in Revelations, capturing the crunchy look of these titles while also putting the gun in the center of the screen. Andrew Hulshult, a notable composer in the boomer-shooter space, will contribute tracks to these segments. 

"And they're cool, too, because it's a little bit different than 2016, where they were very, almost pixel perfect as far as the representation of the graphics," said Stratton. "These are kind of modernized versions of—not modernized from a layout perspective—but taking those original kind of textures and bringing them to a modern engine. It's this awesome balance that I think is super cool."

It's hypothetical at this point, but some segments seem to be focused more on Doom Slayer's story, while others focus more purely on gameplay within these old levels. Martin and Stratton were cryptic about it, but it's sure to have plenty of surprises in store. 

"These are short but meaningful playable experiences," said Martin. "Part of the theme of the narrative approach, and honestly the overall approach to the DLC is we want to, but especially story, is to feel it not watch it. So you will you will experience that part of the story firsthand as you play through what are basically his memories." 

Doom has been and always will be gameplay-first, but the lore was emphasized strongly during the group interview; on that note, Martin said, "We wanted to make this content since Doom 2016," so there seems to be something for every type of fan to enjoy.

With a heavy focus on the Chain Spear and challenging end-game content, on top of exploring more of the lore surrounding the enigmatic Doom Slayer, Doom: The Dark Ages | Revelations is set to release on July 7.

Austin cut his teeth writing various  fan-fiction stories on the RuneScape forums when he was in elementary school. Later on, he developed a deep love… More about Austin
